summary.problem: Pi already has advisory validation scheduling, proof-memory, redundant-work detection, and operator recommendations, but agents still lack a single route-level map from touched paths to proof obligations, RCH-only command templates, cache/coalescing hints, and coordination admission risks.
summary.recommended_track: Build pi.validation.semantic_route_plan.v1 as a read-only advisory layer above existing scheduler, proof-memory, Beads, Agent Mail, git, and runpack evidence.
non_goals: Do not execute cargo or RCH commands.
Do not mutate Beads, Agent Mail, git, RCH, source files, temp artifacts, or evidence sources from the planner.
Do not authorize release performance, capacity, benchmark, or strict drop-in claims.

phase_2_top_5.why: Maps touched paths to exact proof obligations and RCH-only command templates, reducing validation guesswork without replacing existing authorities.

phase_2_top_5.idea: RCH cache-heat and validation coalescing advice
phase_2_top_5.why: Large swarms lose time when agents independently rebuild overlapping targets; route-level cache hints can improve throughput while remaining advisory.
Combines Beads, Agent Mail, git, and redundant-work signals so agents can avoid collision-prone edit surfaces before claiming.
The planner needs fail-closed fixtures for stale proof, RCH pressure, dirty state, and advisory-boundary violations.
Advisory route output is only useful if it appears in the handoff path agents already consult.

phase_3_next_10: README/evidence claim freshness cross-checks for route output
Provider/RPC/TUI route buckets with targeted focused-test suggestions
Extension-runtime route bucket with policy and hostcall fixtures
Closeout evidence registry entry for semantic routing

phase_4_overlap_result.new_surface_boundary: semantic_route_plan should consume and explain these existing sources; it must not duplicate their source-of-truth roles.

phase_6_refinements: Narrowed the first ready item to design/inventory only.
Added implementation dependencies so planner work waits on this inventory.
Separated cache/coalescing advice from proof reuse authority.
Separated coordination admission from Beads and Agent Mail mutation.
Added explicit no-mock/golden evidence and closeout follow-ons.

source_inventory.semantic_route_use: Import command groups, RCH posture, exact command shapes, and no-local-fallback language.
Explain reusable, stale, command-mismatch, path-coverage-mismatch, local-fallback, and non-authoritative proof records.
Reuse safe action names and rejected-alternative patterns for route admission.
Feed collision and duplicate-work risk into route-level admission warnings.
Optional live source for health, active agents, and reservations; missing or degraded data must fail closed or recommend Beads soft-lock.
Ready/in-progress/stalled state, bead thread IDs, and assignee ownership.
RCH-only heavy validation, no deletion, no local fallback, drop-in claim guardrails, and required closeout gates.

proof_group_catalog.local_fallback_rejection_reason: RCH-required validation must not fail open into a local cargo build.

exact_commands: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/pi_agent_rust_cargo/${USER:-agent}/target TMPDIR=/data/tmp/pi_agent_rust_cargo/${USER:-agent}/tmp cargo test conformance
rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/pi_agent_rust_cargo/${USER:-agent}/target TMPDIR=/data/tmp/pi_agent_rust_cargo/${USER:-agent}/tmp cargo test e2e
proof_group_catalog.local_fallback_rejection_reason: RCH-required validation must not fail open into a local cargo build.
RCH-required validation must not fail open into a local cargo build.

exact_commands: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/pi_agent_rust_cargo/${USER:-agent}/target TMPDIR=/data/tmp/pi_agent_rust_cargo/${USER:-agent}/tmp cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
proof_group_catalog.local_fallback_rejection_reason: RCH-required validation must not fail open into a local cargo build.

negative_controls.reason: Route output cannot safely preserve exact validation group semantics without scheduler-plan input or a built-in fallback catalog.
Stale proof-memory may inform route ordering but must not authorize proof reuse.
Heavy cargo validation must be expressed through rch exec -- and denied when RCH posture is unavailable or saturated.
Proof-memory path/git matching is invalid when the current dirty worktree differs from source proof context.
Missing or degraded Agent Mail must not lead to invisible ownership; planner should recommend Beads soft-lock or stop.
Route plans cannot claim beads, reserve files, launch RCH, close work, delete files, or certify release/drop-in claims.
